game_intro: Neuraminidase is the door handle the flu virus uses to escape from your cells. Oseltamivir (Tamiflu) already weakens it — can you build something even better?
boss_flavor: This enzyme has triggered pandemics throughout history. It sits on the viral surface, releasing copies of the virus into your body every second.
plain_english: The flu virus uses this enzyme to copy itself and spread to new cells. Your molecule needs to block it better than Tamiflu.

game_intro: SARS-CoV-2 uses this protease to cut viral proteins into working pieces. Nirmatrelvir (Paxlovid) already weakens it — can you design a better molecule?
boss_flavor: This molecular scissors is critical for COVID-19 replication. Without it, the virus cannot assemble itself. Real drug: Paxlovid (approved 2021).
plain_english: The COVID-19 virus uses this molecular scissors to build itself. Your job is to design a molecule that jams those scissors better than Paxlovid.

game_intro: HIV Protease assembles viral proteins for HIV. Cut off one resistance pathway and it mutates another. No full seed provided — design your own strategy.
boss_flavor: One of the most mutated drug targets in history. HIV constantly evolves new resistance mechanisms — like a hydra that grows back every time you cut a head.
plain_english: HIV needs this enzyme to mature into an infectious virus. Block it and the virus produces harmless, immature particles that cannot infect new cells.

game_intro: EGFR drives uncontrolled cell growth in lung cancer. Gefitinib targets it, but resistance mutations are common. Can you improve on existing drugs?
boss_flavor: This kinase tells cancer cells to keep dividing, no matter what. It is mutated in roughly 15% of non-small cell lung cancers worldwide.
plain_english: EGFR acts as the gas pedal for cancer cell growth. Your molecule needs to remove the foot from the pedal to stop cancer from spreading.

game_intro: BRAF V600E is a drug-resistant mutation in melanoma. The usual drugs no longer work. You need a molecule that specifically targets the mutated form.
boss_flavor: A single amino acid change — V600E — locks this protein permanently in the 'on' position, turning it into a cancer driver. Existing drugs struggle against this mutation.
plain_english: A mutation in BRAF locks the cancer accelerator permanently 'on'. You need a molecule that fits the mutated shape specifically, not the normal shape.

game_intro: SIRT1 is linked to aging and longevity research. This is a frontier target — no approved drug yet. You are working at the very edge of science.
boss_flavor: SIRT1 controls hundreds of genes linked to metabolism, stress response, and aging. Scientists believe activating it may slow the aging process itself.
plain_english: This protein regulates how we age. Designing molecules that interact with it could one day help us live healthier, longer lives — but the science is still being discovered.

game_intro: CDK2 drives the cell division cycle in multiple cancers. Expert level — no hints, AI Army mode recommended. This is the final boss.
boss_flavor: CDK2 is the master timer of cell division. When hijacked by cancer, cells divide endlessly. It is implicated in breast, colon, and many other cancers.
plain_english: CDK2 is the countdown timer that tells cells when to divide. Cancer removes the countdown, so cells divide forever. Stopping CDK2 restores the timer.
